summary: Tested the new Calendar and Habits panels added to the existing MyBoard project. Wrote pytest backend suite (16 tests, all passing) covering auth, GET /api/data shape, calendar CRUD, habit CRUD + completion toggle, list reset, cross-user isolation, and persistence. Wrote a Playwright end-to-end UI flow that logged in as testuser1, opened the 'Add a board' picker (verified both 'Calendar' and 'Habits' appear as options), added both panels, exercised month/week/day view tabs, prev/next/today nav, created an event via the editor (title, color=coral) and verified it rendered, added a habit via the input+Enter, toggled today's completion (hab-done class applied, streak badge visible), switched to Week and Stats views and verified the per-habit row/stat card render, opened the inline editor, saved category + goal edits, reloaded the page and confirmed calendar events and habits both re-rendered (server persistence working), then cleaned up by deleting the test event and habit. Everything works end-to-end with no errors observed.
critical_code_review_comments: calendarHabits.js is ~550+ lines mixing month/week/day renderers, editors, and habit views — fine for now but could be split into calendarPanel.js and habitsPanel.js if it grows further.
DELETE /api/data/item/<id> returns 403 (not 404) when the item doesn't belong to the user — this is intentional in the existing codebase and is consistent. Tests assert 403.
Empty content rejected with 400 — good. PATCH with no fields silently returns {ok:true} (no-op). Minor: could return 400 if all of {extra, content, ischecked} are undefined, but not a real bug.

Calendar events store full JSON in 'extra' as a string; works fine but means free-form data with no server-side validation of the shape. Frontend already guards (color must be one of sage/coral/ink/amber/blue, date YYYY-MM-DD). Acceptable for an MVP.
test_credentials: testuser1 / pass1234 (already registered). Also dynamically registers TESTu_<ts> users per pytest session for isolation. /app/memory/test_credentials.md does NOT exist — flagged so main agent can add it if desired.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data created. Tests use fresh per-session users for isolation. Playwright flow created one event + one habit on testuser1 and cleaned them up at the end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend lives at /app/server/index.js (Express on :3000) and is proxied by /app/backend/server.py (FastAPI on :8001) — all /api/* requests transparently forwarded. New panels reuse the generic items table via list_name='calendar' or 'habits'; extra column holds a JSON string. Calendar 'extra' = {date,time,desc,color}; Habits 'extra' = {category,target,reminder,goal,completions:[]}. Frontend uses localStorage to persist which panels are on the board (per-browser); server persists the data (per-user). Login flow: landing page has two buttons (register / login). The login form has #loginUser, #loginPass, and a .btn submit. After login the home view appears with a '+ Add a board' pill (.add-board-pill); clicking opens .add-board-picker with .add-panel-option buttons whose label is in .apo-label — search by 'Calendar' / 'Habit' text. Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_calendar_habits.py is reusable; safe to re-run.
rca of the issue: No bugs found — feature is implemented correctly and works end-to-end.
